purpose: Independent verification of externally-published claims (Tibane Labs) about SecondFi/Yoroi root cause and supply-chain provenance. Collected by BEACNpool audit maintainers, not by Tibane Labs.

npm_registry_stashers_trantor.maintainer_email_domain_note: resolved from _resolved/_from local path '/var/home/boris/Public/trantor/releases/trantor/_local/package.tgz' in registry metadata
npm_registry_stashers_trantor.result: CONFIRMED: single published version 0.5.2, no repository/homepage field, dependencies include @noble/curves + @noble/hashes + @noble/ciphers as claimed.

github_repo_stashers-io_trantor.result: CONFIRMED: repository not publicly accessible, consistent with Tibane's claim of a DMCA-driven takedown.

github_user_boris-kolar.public_repos_visible_via_api: 0 (per Tibane report; account exists but repos_url yields none public)
github_user_boris-kolar.result: Account exists publicly; consistent with Tibane's footprint table. Identity of X account @boris_kolar as same person is NOT independently verified by us or by Tibane (both label it attributed-only).

github_dmca_archive_emurgo_takedown.claim_summary: Filer identifies itself as authorized representative of EMURGO Group Pte. Ltd., 'the rightful owner of proprietary software project Dullahan', names https://github.com/stashers-io/trantor as an unauthorized copy created by 'one of our former employees', requests takedown of the entire repository.
github_dmca_archive_emurgo_takedown.result: CONFIRMED verbatim against Tibane's supply-chain report §04/§10[15]: EMURGO's own filing names the code 'Dullahan' and asserts sole ownership, disputing only that a former employee published it without authorization.

github_dmca_archive_counternotice.claim_summary: Counter-filer disputes only licensing (claims MIT LICENSE.txt was present in the repo since 2024-06-06 with branch protections preventing unilateral addition), and states some contributed code predated the EMURGO contract. Does NOT dispute that the code is/was EMURGO's Dullahan project.
github_dmca_archive_counternotice.result: CONFIRMED verbatim against Tibane's supply-chain report §04/§10[16]: dispute is licensing-only, not origin.

blockfrost_tx_4655145484...ce02203.result: CONFIRMED: transaction Tibane cites as the tx that reproduces their predicted-nonce R value exists on Cardano mainnet, dated 2026-06-10 -- inside the claimed 2026-06-08..2026-06-25 exposure window. We did not independently recompute the R-value/nonce math; we only confirmed the transaction's on-chain existence and timestamp.

not_independently_verified: The Ed25519 nonce-recovery math itself (r = SHA-512(M), single-signature key recovery) -- cryptographically plausible and internally consistent with RFC 8032, but we did not re-derive private scalars ourselves.
The claimed 221/248 (89%) identifier match between trantor 0.5.2 source and the decompiled SecondFi Android build 10.0.3 -- we did not obtain or decompile the APK ourselves.
Identity of the X account @boris_kolar as the actual SDK author -- Tibane itself flags this as attributed, not confirmed, and so do we.
Aggregate victim/loss figures on the tibane.net page -- labeled by Tibane as OSINT-sourced, not chain-verified by them or by us.
